name: Misal Pav
description: A spicy Kolhapuri-style misal made with sprouted moth bean usal, a roasted coconut and spice vatan paste, fiery tarri, and classic toppings like farsan, onions, coriander, and lemon.
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, finely chopped, for usal
1 tablespoon oil, for whole spice base
2 tablespoons oil, for frying onion-tomato base
1 large onion, finely chopped, for tarri
Farsan or sev/misal mix, for garnish

itemListElement.text: Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a pressure cooker.
Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add cumin seeds, curry leaves, and hing. Fry briefly until aromatic.
Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook briefly until the raw smell reduces.
Add chopped tomato, turmeric powder, and salt. Cook until the tomato softens.
Add the sprouted moth beans and mix well.
Add enough water to cover the beans.
Close the pressure cooker and cook for 1 whistle. Set the usal aside.
Dry roast the desiccated coconut until golden brown. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan.
Add cumin seeds, shah jeera, coriander seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, black cardamom, black peppercorns, and cloves. Roast until fragrant.
Add ginger, garlic, sesame seeds, poppy seeds, and fresh coconut. Roast lightly, then set aside.
In another pan, heat 2 tablespoons oil and shallow fry the sliced onions until golden brown.
Add diced tomatoes and a pinch of salt. Cook until the tomatoes soften and melt.
Let the roasted coconut, whole spice mixture, and onion-tomato mixture cool.
Add all three vatan components to a blender with minimal water and grind into a smooth paste.
Heat 5 to 6 tablespoons oil in a pan.
Add the finely chopped onion and fry until golden brown.
Add Kashmiri red chili powder, regular red chili powder, and goda masala. Fry briefly, taking care not to burn the spices.
Add the prepared vatan paste and mix well.
Cook on medium heat for about 8 minutes, until the oil separates, the color deepens, and the texture thickens.
Add hot usal to a serving bowl.
Pour the spicy tarri on top.
Garnish with finely chopped onions, farsan or sev/misal mix, and fresh coriander.
Add a squeeze of lemon if desired.
Serve hot with pav bread rolls or plain rice.
